name: Alan Rubin
abstract: Alan Rubin (February 11, 1943 – June 8, 2011) was an American musician. He played trumpet, flugelhorn, and piccolo trumpet.

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Malone
about_quotes.evidence: We had the band with Lou Marini, Alan Rubin, Howard Johnson, Paul Shaffer... The horn section was Alan Rubin, Lou Marini, Tom Scott, and myself.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom Malone interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 3, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Fred Lipsius
about_quotes.evidence: those two guys, somewhere during the time I was with Blood, Sweat & Tears, they said, “You’re a natural, Fred.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Fred Lipsius interviewed by Monk Rowe. Boston, Massachusetts, July 25, 2018

about_quotes.speaker: Lou Marini
about_quotes.evidence: Alan Rubin, my colleague in the Blues Brothers Band and for so many sessions and stuff in New York
about_quotes.doc_title: Lou Marini interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 15, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Palmieri
about_quotes.evidence: I already had recorded Jon Faddis, Lew Soloff, Chocolate, and Alan Rubin on the danzon album that went into the can.
about_quotes.doc_title: Eddie Palmieri — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
